real_insight: When a senior on-call freezes mid-incident and starts apologising instead of executing the runbook, the corrective is to name the freeze and point at the next runbook step — console after the page is closed.
objection: I came in expecting fortune-cookie material and instead got a specific named scene: 2:47 AM, partial Postgres migration, twelve of seventeen tables, anguished apologies in the incident channel. The insight is precise: self-recrimination at viṣama-kāla is desertion in apology's clothes. That is exactly the move I have watched senior engineers make and not had a name for. The verse explicitly anticipates the failure mode I would test it with — using it to lecture juniors — and disclaims that use. Wisdom-theater detector reads near-zero. The only thing that would push this into theater is the line 'desertion in apology's clothes' if pulled out of context, but in context it does load-bearing work.
